assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_003",
  "rule_id": "Set kernel parameter 'crypto.fips_enabled' to 1",
  "category": "too_dangerous_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.97,
  "rationale": "Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the fips=1 kernel boot parameter, which modifies the bootloader configuration. Changing this on an already installed system is not supported and can render the system unbootable or cause kernel failures, posing a high risk of loss of access.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "system may fail to boot",
    "potential kernel panic",
    "loss of console/SSH access if boot fails",
    "requires reboot"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Verify whether FIPS mode is required for the environment",
    "If required, plan a reinstall or re-deployment with the fips=1 boot parameter set during installation",
    "Consult documentation and change management before modifying bootloader settings"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": true,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
assistant_message.reasoning:
We need to classify. Finding: set kernel param crypto.fips_enabled to 1. Description says enabling FIPS on preexisting system is not supported; requires reinstall or boot with fips=1 during install. Changing boot parameters may have serious impact. This touches kernel boot parameters (bootloader). Policy says mark too_dangerous_to_remediate ONLY for partitioning/filesystem/bootloader/FIPS changes. This is a FIPS change to kernel boot param, which is bootloader. So likely too_dangerous_to_remediate. However, the policy also says prefer safe_to_remediate when unlikely to cause lockout; but they specifically say mark too_dangerous for bootloader/FIPS changes. So category: too_dangerous_to_remediate.

Set confidence maybe high 0.97.

rationale: Changing boot parameter requires editing GRUB, could break system if not reinstall; not supported.

risk_factors: ["system may fail to boot", "loss of FIPS compliance", "potential kernel panic", "requires reboot"].

safe_next_steps: ["Review system installation method", "If FIPS required, reinstall with fips=1 kernel option", "Consult security policy"].

requires_reboot: true (boot param change requires reboot). touches_authn_authz: false. touches_networking: false. touches_filesystems: false.

Return JSON.
